channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Gustave Actor Wants Robert Pattinson in Expedition 33 Film
item.content:encoded:
The actor who played Gustave in Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 has named the actor he would like to see portray the character on the big screen. However, there’s a catch. He still wants to provide the voice.

In 2025, Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 captured the hearts and minds of players. The Sandfall Interactive title set a new standard for AA video games and what “independent” games can produce. A large part of that was due to the portrayal of the characters by the talented performers who gave voice to the story.

Charlie Cox Would Like to See Robert Pattinson in a Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 Movie

Charlie Cox received critical acclaim for his portrayal of Gustave. Part of that acclaim included the game winning another Game of the Year award at the 2026 BAFTA Games Awards. Ahead of the event, Cox was asked by GamesRadar+ if he’d like to reprise his role one day.

Cox says he would absolutely be down for such a moment. Still, he believes that if the game were to be made into a movie, the part should be performed by Robert Pattinson, with the catch that he would still provide the voice.

“My involvement with this, as minuscule as it was originally and how it’s now expanded so much, has been nothing but an incredibly humbling and enjoyable experience,” he tells us. “So, yeah, absolutely, yeah. I was thinking that they should probably – if they make the movie – they should get Robert Pattinson to play the part, and I should do the voice. Because then they would have pretty much the exact Gustave from the game.”

There you have it. The “Twilight” and “The Batman” star now has the endorsement of Cox to play Gustave on the big screen.

To check out the full list of winners from the 2026 BAFTA Games Awards, click here.

Would you like to see a movie about Expedition 33? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: NYT Strands #777 Hints and Answers for Today, April 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #777 for Saturday, April 19, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, April 19, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #777 are all related to terms you use to describe small changes.

General Strands Hints For April 19

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: A word that means to do better.
  • Hint 2: An anagram of “later.”
  • Hint 3: Steam supports a community that does this to games.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #777 for April 19, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for April 18, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • DOME
  • DOMAIN
  • MAIN
  • FIXED
  • THER

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #777 On April 19, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • ALTER
  • MODIFY
  • IMPROVE
  • REFINE
  • TWEAK
  • ADJUST
  • SPANAGRAM: THEREIFIXEDIT

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #777?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on April 20 will be Strands Game #778.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Metro Creator Doesn’t Think 2039 Should Be ‘Called a Game’
item.content:encoded:
Earlier this week, Metro 2039 was unveiled by 4A Games after years of development. The next-generation, post-apocalyptic title promises to give players a dark, disturbing journey back into Moscow’s dank underground, having been heavily influenced by the ongoing war in Ukraine.

The creator of the Metro franchise, Dmitry Glukhovsky, has wasted no time promoting Metro 2039 on social media. Most recently, he stressed that the all-new title should transcend being just ‘a game’.

Metro 2039 Promises To Be The Darkest Game Ever

Metro 2039 has the real potential to be one of the darkest games we’ve ever seen released into the mainstream space. The popular series, which started in 2010 with Metro 2033, has long toyed with sensitive topics such as slavery, genocide, war, torture, and psychological torment, but Metro 2039 will go leagues beyond that.

From the game’s first trailer, we know exactly how morbid 2039 will be. The opening walkthrough of the game’s core concepts was punctuated by first-hand accounts from the team at 4A Games, who said that suffering at the hands of Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine led them to shape 2039 into something impossibly dark.

In a recent statement on social media, Dmitry Glukhovsky, the author of the Metro novels upon which the video game franchise is based, suggested that these themes and the story presented in 2039 make it more than just a game:

I don’t think Metro 2039 should be called a game. It should be called an experience.

Earlier, he wrote:

At dark times, at times of war, stories need to mirror the reality, to register it – so that the lessons of these times are never forgotten. Metro 2039 is a such a mirror. But, as expected of mirrors, you will see your own reflection when you look at it. Who are you?

In recent days, Glukhovsky has dubbed Metro 2039 ‘the most grown-up story’, stressing that it ‘doesn’t seem to be a dystopia or science-fiction any more.’

Glukhovsky has always remained very close to the development of the Metro series, which has rendered the franchise deeply political over the years. In 2024, Metro Awakening, the last game in the series and a VR spin-off, was released, with Glukhovsky revealing that he wrote the game’s story firsthand.

In 2025, he admitted that Metro 2039’s story, which at the time was kept under lock and key, had given him ‘goosebumps’.

It follows the cancellation of a previous version of Metro 2039, which was scrapped in 2022 when Russia mounted a full-scale invasion of Ukraine. The team at 4A Games opted to pivot to this refreshed version to portray better the real-world impact of the situation on their development circle.

We all heard the stories of 4A Games developing Metro 2039 while sheltering from incoming missiles in bunkers across Ukraine. Even though the studio is now split, with a major office having been opened in Malta, Kyiv remains a core hub for the development team.

Do you think Metro 2039 will be impressive enough to deliver on claims that it’s ‘an experience’ rather than just ‘a game’? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that new ARC Raiders map teasers have been posted

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1043)—Answers for Sunday, April 19
item.content:encoded:
It’s a quiet Sunday, but before you can get into your plans, you need to complete today’s NYT Connections puzzle. If you want to do that as quickly as possible, I’ve got all the answers and hints you need below. Let’s help you get your day started the right way; simply scroll down to access today’s NYT Connections solutions fast.

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (April 17)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • KID
  • RIVER
  • BUST
  • SASSY
  • MINT
  • FLOP
  • WISE
  • HIPS
  • DUD
  • TURN
  • CAP
  • FRESH
  • WAIST
  • HOLE
  • ARCH
  • LENGTH

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Words you’d use to replace ‘cheeky’
  • Green – Words you’d associate with measurements in tailoring
  • Blue – Words you’d associate with cards in Texas Hold’em
  • Purple – Words you’d find on popular candy bars

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, April 17:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, April 17 (#1041)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – ARCH, FRESH, SASSY, WISE
  • Green – BUST, HIPS, LENGTH, WAIST
  • Blue – FLOP, HOLE, RIVER, TURN
  • Purple – CAP, DUD, KID, MINT

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill
